top_3_actions: Trust the car's grip — it has more than you're using. Carry more entry speed into corners, hold throttle longer on exit, and brake later/harder to spend more time near the grip limit.
[Straight_2] Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.
[Straight_4] Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

verdicts.finding: Only 9.4% of cornering time near grip limit (peak: 1.67g, target: >25% of corners at ≥80% peak g).
verdicts.reasoning: During corners and braking, the car reaches 1.67g peak, but spends only 9.4% of that time above 80% of its own peak. This means most corners are being driven well below the car's capability — either entry speed is too low, apex is taken too cautiously, or exits are lifted early.
verdicts.action: Trust the car's grip — it has more than you're using. Carry more entry speed into corners, hold throttle longer on exit, and brake later/harder to spend more time near the grip limit.

verdicts.finding: Only 75% of straight at full throttle (expected >89% for 387m straight).
verdicts.reasoning: On a 387m straight entered at 148 km/h, the car spent 25% of the distance with partial throttle, losing potential acceleration time.
verdicts.action: Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

verdicts.finding: Only 76% of straight at full throttle (expected >84% for 321m straight).
verdicts.reasoning: On a 321m straight entered at 142 km/h, the car spent 24% of the distance with partial throttle, losing potential acceleration time.
verdicts.action: Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

verdicts.finding: Rear wheelspin detected on corner exit.
verdicts.reasoning: The rear tires exceeded their traction limit during acceleration. Spinning tires generate less forward force than rolling tires, so the car actually accelerates slower despite more throttle input. It also risks snap oversteer.
verdicts.action: Apply throttle more progressively on exit. Wait until the steering is unwinding before increasing throttle past 50%. Think: 'unwind the wheel, then add throttle' — not both at once.

verdicts.finding: Trail-brake release quality: R²=0.76 (target: >0.85). Brake pressure decay is not smooth.
verdicts.reasoning: An R² of 0.76 means the brake release is inconsistent — the pressure jumps or plateaus instead of declining linearly. This causes abrupt weight transfer changes mid-corner, reducing front grip predictability.
verdicts.action: Focus on a smooth, progressive brake release through the corner entry. Imagine slowly lifting your foot off a feather. The brake trace should look like a clean downward ramp.
